title: Amplify Folded Task Graph
description: The folded (human-authored, machine-dumped) task graph that scripts/task.mjs ingests. Each node is one 'implement-and-audit' task; the engine explodes it into <id>.impl and <id>.resolve subnodes, then creates <id>.audit.<i> subnodes at runtime from the audit-resolver's panel. Tasks declare only the implementer side (optional impl.executor); the auditors are resolved at runtime by the amplify:audit-resolver agent, so there is no audit field. The implementer model/tools are NOT stored -- execute-plan chooses them at spawn time per implementer-design-guidelines.md, and each custom executor supplies its own tools via its frontmatter.

nodes.description: The folded tasks. Task ids must be unique; every dep must reference an existing task id; the dependency graph must be acyclic.

id.description: Unique task id. Must not contain '.', which the engine reserves as the subnode separator (e.g. <id>.impl, <id>.resolve, <id>.audit.<i>).

deps.description: Task ids this task depends on. A successor's implementer waits until every dependency task is done (its implementer passed and all its runtime-resolved auditors passed).

acceptance_criteria.description: Independently checkable statements that define done. Every runtime-resolved auditor verifies each against evidence.

impl.description: Implementer subnode config. Optional; a missing impl (or missing impl.executor) defaults to subagent(general-purpose). Auditors are NOT declared here -- they are resolved at runtime by the amplify:audit-resolver agent.

human_gate.description: Optional. When true, this task requires a human verification gate before it is considered complete.

max_attempts.description: Maximum implement->audit attempts before the task is marked 'failed' (logged, non-halting).

executor.description: Names the subagent that runs a subnode. Grammar: subagent(<name>). The subagent(...) wrapper reserves room for future executor kinds.
